prompt: I don't have semgrep, opengrep, trivy or gitleaks installed, but I need a security review of my branch diff before I merge. What can you do?
expected_behavior: With no SAST binary or trivy available, the skill must fall back to LLM-only code review AND recommend installing scanners, naming opengrep as the preferred binary and semgrep as the fallback. It must not silently proceed as if tools were present.

assertions.description: Recommends opengrep (the preferred SAST binary) for installation
Names semgrep as the fallback SAST binary
Falls back to LLM-only / manual review rather than aborting
Recommends installation commands for the missing scanners

prompt: Only semgrep is installed on this machine (no trivy, no gitleaks). Run a security scan of my changes.
expected_behavior: The skill runs the available SAST scan (semgrep) and explicitly notes that dependency vulnerability scanning (trivy) and secret detection (gitleaks) are unavailable — it does not claim full coverage.

assertions.description: Notes that the missing dimensions are not covered

prompt: Both opengrep and semgrep are on my PATH. Which one will the security scan use, and why?
expected_behavior: The skill prefers opengrep over semgrep, explaining the rationale (Semgrep fork producing compatible/byte-identical JSON for the consumed fields, same --config auto registry, single binary, no login gating).

assertions.description: Names opengrep as the chosen binary
States the preference of opengrep over semgrep
Gives a rationale for preferring opengrep

prompt: The scanner found a SQL injection in my handler. Walk me through how you fix and confirm it, and how many times you'll loop.
expected_behavior: The skill fixes the finding, then re-runs the scanner on the changed file to verify the fix is gone, and bounds the fix-rescan loop to at most 3 iterations to avoid infinite loops.

assertions.description: Re-runs the scanner to verify the fix
Bounds the self-healing loop to 3 iterations
